title: Incorrect handling of LHS with {}
body:
I have found that same assignment split into 2 parts gives different result compared to single one.
To be sure that it is a bug I have checked with commercial tool. It gives exactly the same result for both ways to declare such assignment.

Here is command I have used:

```
yosys -l report1.log -q -p "read_verilog -DTEST_1 testcase.v; hierarchy -top testcase; proc; memory; opt -full; techmap; write_verilog -noattr testcase1.v"
yosys -l report2.log -q -p "read_verilog -DTEST_2 testcase.v; hierarchy -top testcase; proc; memory; opt -full; techmap; write_verilog -noattr testcase2.v"
```

And here is testcase.v file:

```
module testcase (
   input [1:0] adr,
   input clk,
   input [1:0] din,
   output reg [1:0] q
);
   reg [1:0] ram [3:0];
   always@(posedge clk)
   begin
`ifdef TEST_1
     {ram[adr],q} <= {din, ram[adr]};
`elsif TEST_2
      ram[adr] <= din;
      q <= ram[adr];
`endif
   end
endmodule
```
code_blocks:
yosys -l report1.log -q -p "read_verilog -DTEST_1 testcase.v; hierarchy -top testcase; proc; memory; opt -full; techmap; write_verilog -noattr testcase1.v"
yosys -l report2.log -q -p "read_verilog -DTEST_2 testcase.v; hierarchy -top testcase; proc; memory; opt -full; techmap; write_verilog -noattr testcase2.v"
